sections: Field hockey at the Summer Olympics
first_paragraph: [Field hockey](/wiki/Field_hockey "Field hockey") made its [debut](/wiki/Field_hockey_at_the_1908_Summer_Olympics "Field hockey at the 1908 Summer Olympics") at the [Modern Olympic Games](/wiki/Modern_Olympic_Games "Modern Olympic Games") as a men's competition in the [1908 Games](/wiki/1908_Summer_Olympics "1908 Summer Olympics") in [London](/wiki/London "London"). It was removed from the Olympic schedule of the [Summer Olympic Games](/wiki/Summer_Olympic_Games "Summer Olympic Games") for the [1924 Paris Games](/wiki/1924_Summer_Olympics "1924 Summer Olympics") and was reintroduced in the [1928 Amsterdam Games](/wiki/1928_Summer_Olympics "1928 Summer Olympics"). The Women's field hockey was [introduced](/wiki/Field_hockey_at_the_1980_Summer_Olympics_%E2%80%93_Women%27s_tournament "Field hockey at the 1980 Summer Olympics – Women's tournament") into the Olympic programme at the [1980 Moscow Olympics](/wiki/1980_Moscow_Olympics "1980 Moscow Olympics").

[Field hockey](/wiki/Field_hockey "Field hockey") made its [debut](/wiki/Field_hockey_at_the_1908_Summer_Olympics "Field hockey at the 1908 Summer Olympics") at the [Modern Olympic Games](/wiki/Modern_Olympic_Games "Modern Olympic Games") as a men's competition in the [1908 Games](/wiki/1908_Summer_Olympics "1908 Summer Olympics") in [London](/wiki/London "London"). It was removed from the Olympic schedule of the [Summer Olympic Games](/wiki/Summer_Olympic_Games "Summer Olympic Games") for the [1924 Paris Games](/wiki/1924_Summer_Olympics "1924 Summer Olympics") and was reintroduced in the [1928 Amsterdam Games](/wiki/1928_Summer_Olympics "1928 Summer Olympics"). The Women's field hockey was [introduced](/wiki/Field_hockey_at_the_1980_Summer_Olympics_%E2%80%93_Women%27s_tournament "Field hockey at the 1980 Summer Olympics – Women's tournament") into the Olympic programme at the [1980 Moscow Olympics](/wiki/1980_Moscow_Olympics "1980 Moscow Olympics").

The [International Hockey Federation](/wiki/International_Hockey_Federation "International Hockey Federation") (FIH) was founded in 1924 and governs the field hockey events. Until the [1988 Summer Olympics](/wiki/1988_Summer_Olympics "1988 Summer Olympics"), the tournaments were invitational, but a qualification system was introduced since the [1992 Barcelona Olympics](/wiki/Field_Hockey_at_the_1992_Summer_Olympics "Field Hockey at the 1992 Summer Olympics"). The last edition at [Paris](/wiki/2024_Paris_Olympics "2024 Paris Olympics") was the 25th occurrence of the [men's event](/wiki/Field_hockey_at_the_2024_Summer_Olympics_%E2%80%93_Men%27s_tournament "Field hockey at the 2024 Summer Olympics – Men's tournament") and 12th consecutive occurrence of [women's event](/wiki/Field_hockey_at_the_2024_Summer_Olympics_%E2%80%93_Women%27s_tournament "Field hockey at the 2024 Summer Olympics – Women's tournament") in the Olympics.

India holds the record for the most appearances in the Olympic men's competitions (22) while Australia and Netherlands hold the same record in women's competitions (11). India leads the medal tally for the men's events with 13 medals while the Netherlands heads the women's events with 10 medals.

[Great Britain](/wiki/Great_Britain_men%27s_national_field_hockey_team "Great Britain men's national field hockey team") won the first two editions of the men's event in [1908](/wiki/Field_hockey_at_the_1908_Summer_Olympics "Field hockey at the 1908 Summer Olympics") and [1920](/wiki/Field_hockey_at_the_1920_Summer_Olympics "Field hockey at the 1920 Summer Olympics"). [India](/wiki/India_men%27s_national_field_hockey_team "India men's national field hockey team") won the gold medal in seven out of eight Olympics from [1928](/wiki/Field_hockey_at_the_1928_Summer_Olympics "Field hockey at the 1928 Summer Olympics") to [1964](/wiki/Field_hockey_at_the_1964_Summer_Olympics "Field hockey at the 1964 Summer Olympics") with [Pakistan](/wiki/Pakistan_men%27s_national_field_hockey_team "Pakistan men's national field hockey team") winning three gold and silver medals each between the 1956 and [1984 Games](/wiki/Field_hockey_at_the_1984_Summer_Olympics "Field hockey at the 1984 Summer Olympics"). The matches are played on [artificial turf](/wiki/Artificial_turf "Artificial turf") since [1976](/wiki/1976_Summer_Olympics "1976 Summer Olympics"). Since the late 1980s, European nations have dominated the field hockey events with [Germany](/wiki/Germany_men%27s_national_field_hockey_team "Germany men's national field hockey team") and [Netherlands](/wiki/Netherlands_men%27s_national_field_hockey_team "Netherlands men's national field hockey team") having won three gold medals each in the men's event. In the women's event, [Netherlands](/wiki/Netherlands_women%27s_national_field_hockey_team "Netherlands women's national field hockey team") and [Australia](/wiki/Australia_women%27s_national_field_hockey_team "Australia women's national field hockey team") have been dominant, having won five and three gold medals respectively since its introduction in 1980.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Field_hockey_at_the_Summer_Olympics&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

[Field hockey](/wiki/Field_hockey "Field hockey") was introduced at the [Modern Olympic Games](/wiki/Modern_Olympic_Games "Modern Olympic Games") as a men's competition at the [1908 Games](/wiki/1908_Summer_Olympics "1908 Summer Olympics") in [London](/wiki/London "London"). [Great Britain](/wiki/Great_Britain_men%27s_national_field_hockey_team "Great Britain men's national field hockey team") won the first two editions in [1908](/wiki/Field_hockey_at_the_1908_Summer_Olympics "Field hockey at the 1908 Summer Olympics") (as [England](/wiki/England_men%27s_national_field_hockey_team "England men's national field hockey team")) and [1920](/wiki/Field_hockey_at_the_1920_Summer_Olympics "Field hockey at the 1920 Summer Olympics").[[1]](#cite_note-NBC-1) It was removed from the Olympic schedule of the [Summer Olympic Games](/wiki/Summer_Olympic_Games "Summer Olympic Games") for the [1924 Paris Games](/wiki/1924_Summer_Olympics "1924 Summer Olympics") because of the lack of an international sporting structure. The [International Hockey Federation](/wiki/International_Hockey_Federation "International Hockey Federation") (FIH) was founded in Paris that year as a response to the same.[[2]](#cite_note-2) Men's field hockey was added to the Olympics for the next Olympic Games in [1928 Games](/wiki/1928_Summer_Olympics "1928 Summer Olympics") held at [Amsterdam](/wiki/Amsterdam "Amsterdam").[[1]](#cite_note-NBC-1)[[3]](#cite_note-Olympedia-3)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/8e/Indian-Hockey-Team-Berlin-1936.jpg/250px-Indian-Hockey-Team-Berlin-1936.jpg)](/wiki/File:Indian-Hockey-Team-Berlin-1936.jpg)

[Indian team](/wiki/India_men%27s_national_field_hockey_team "India men's national field hockey team") that won the gold at the [1936 Berlin Olympics](/wiki/India_at_the_1936_Summer_Olympics "India at the 1936 Summer Olympics")

Starting in [1928](/wiki/Field_hockey_at_the_1928_Summer_Olympics "Field hockey at the 1928 Summer Olympics"), [India](/wiki/India_men%27s_national_field_hockey_team "India men's national field hockey team") won the gold medal in seven out of eight Olympics till [1964](/wiki/Field_hockey_at_the_1964_Summer_Olympics "Field hockey at the 1964 Summer Olympics") including six consecutive gold medals from the [1928 Olympics](/wiki/1928_Summer_Olympics "1928 Summer Olympics") to [1956](/wiki/Field_hockey_at_the_1956_Summer_Olympics "Field hockey at the 1956 Summer Olympics").[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-India-5) [Pakistan](/wiki/Pakistan_men%27s_national_field_hockey_team "Pakistan men's national field hockey team") won its first gold medal in [1960](/wiki/Field_hockey_at_the_1960_Summer_Olympics "Field hockey at the 1960 Summer Olympics") and won three gold and silver medals each in a run lasting from 1956 to [1984](/wiki/Field_hockey_at_the_1984_Summer_Olympics "Field hockey at the 1984 Summer Olympics").[[6]](#cite_note-Pakistan-6) [West Germany](/wiki/West_Germany_men%27s_national_field_hockey_team "West Germany men's national field hockey team") won the gold medal in the [1972 Munich Olympics](/wiki/Field_hockey_at_the_1972_Summer_Olympics "Field hockey at the 1972 Summer Olympics"), for the first gold medal by a non-Asian country since 1928. Olympic field hockey games were first played on [artificial turf](/wiki/Artificial_turf "Artificial turf") at the [1976 Montreal Games](/wiki/1976_Summer_Olympics "1976 Summer Olympics") and was won by [New Zealand](/wiki/New_Zealand_men%27s_national_field_hockey_team "New Zealand men's national field hockey team").[[1]](#cite_note-NBC-1)[[7]](#cite_note-7) The domination of both the Asian countries came to an end in the late 1980s with India winning its last gold medal in the competition in [1980](/wiki/Field_hockey_at_the_1980_Summer_Olympics "Field hockey at the 1980 Summer Olympics") and Pakistan in 1984.[[5]](#cite_note-India-5)[[6]](#cite_note-Pakistan-6)

Though the International Federation of Women’s Hockey Associations (IFWHA) was founded way back in 1927, [Women's field hockey](/wiki/Field_hockey_at_the_1980_Summer_Olympics_%E2%80%93_Women%27s_tournament "Field hockey at the 1980 Summer Olympics – Women's tournament") made its debut at the [Summer Olympics](/wiki/Summer_Olympics "Summer Olympics") only at the [1980 Moscow Olympics](/wiki/1980_Moscow_Olympics "1980 Moscow Olympics") and was won by [Zimbabwe](/wiki/Zimbabwe_women%27s_national_field_hockey_team "Zimbabwe women's national field hockey team"). On 23 April 1983, women’s hockey programme was taken over by FIH and the IFWHA was dissolved.[[3]](#cite_note-Olympedia-3) Great Britain won its first gold medal in the [men's event](/wiki/Field_hockey_at_the_1988_Summer_Olympics_%E2%80%93_Men%27s_tournament "Field hockey at the 1988 Summer Olympics – Men's tournament") since 1920 in the [1988 Seoul Olympics](/wiki/1988_Seoul_Olympics "1988 Seoul Olympics").[[1]](#cite_note-NBC-1)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/c3/Netherlands_womens_hockey_celebrate_-_2012_Olympics.jpg/250px-Netherlands_womens_hockey_celebrate_-_2012_Olympics.jpg)](/wiki/File:Netherlands_womens_hockey_celebrate_-_2012_Olympics.jpg)

[Netherlands women's team](/wiki/Netherlands_women%27s_national_field_hockey_team "Netherlands women's national field hockey team") after winning gold at the [2012 Olympics](/wiki/Field_hockey_at_the_2012_Summer_Olympics_%E2%80%93_Women%27s_tournament "Field hockey at the 2012 Summer Olympics – Women's tournament")

Until the 1988 Olympics, the tournament was invitational but FIH introduced a qualification system starting from the [1992 Barcelona Olympics](/wiki/Field_Hockey_at_the_1992_Summer_Olympics "Field Hockey at the 1992 Summer Olympics"). Since the 1990s, European countries have been dominant in the men's sport with the only exceptions being the wins by [Australia](/wiki/Australia_men%27s_national_field_hockey_team "Australia men's national field hockey team") in [2004](/wiki/Field_hockey_at_the_2004_Summer_Olympics_%E2%80%93_Men%27s_tournament "Field hockey at the 2004 Summer Olympics – Men's tournament") and [Argentina](/wiki/Argentina_men%27s_national_field_hockey_team "Argentina men's national field hockey team") in [2016](/wiki/Field_hockey_at_the_2016_Summer_Olympics_%E2%80%93_Men%27s_tournament "Field hockey at the 2016 Summer Olympics – Men's tournament"). Both [Germany](/wiki/Germany_men%27s_national_field_hockey_team "Germany men's national field hockey team") and [Netherlands](/wiki/Netherlands_men%27s_national_field_hockey_team "Netherlands men's national field hockey team") have won three gold medals each, with the only other European winner being [Belgium](/wiki/Belgium_men%27s_national_field_hockey_team "Belgium men's national field hockey team") in [2020](/wiki/Field_hockey_at_the_2020_Summer_Olympics_%E2%80%93_Men%27s_tournament "Field hockey at the 2020 Summer Olympics – Men's tournament").[[1]](#cite_note-NBC-1) In the women's events, [Netherlands](/wiki/Netherlands_women%27s_national_field_hockey_team "Netherlands women's national field hockey team") and [Australia](/wiki/Australia_women%27s_national_field_hockey_team "Australia women's national field hockey team") have been dominant, having won five and three gold medals respectively since its introduction to the Olympic programme in [1980](/wiki/1980_Summer_Olympics "1980 Summer Olympics"). The only other winners of the women's event were [Spain](/wiki/Spain_women%27s_national_field_hockey_team "Spain women's national field hockey team") in [1992](/wiki/Field_hockey_at_the_1992_Summer_Olympics_%E2%80%93_Women%27s_tournament "Field hockey at the 1992 Summer Olympics – Women's tournament"), [Germany](/wiki/Germany_women%27s_national_field_hockey_team "Germany women's national field hockey team") in [2004](/wiki/Field_hockey_at_the_2004_Summer_Olympics_%E2%80%93_Women%27s_tournament "Field hockey at the 2004 Summer Olympics – Women's tournament"), and [Great Britain](/wiki/Great_Britain_women%27s_national_field_hockey_team "Great Britain women's national field hockey team") in [2016](/wiki/Field_hockey_at_the_2016_Summer_Olympics_%E2%80%93_Women%27s_tournament "Field hockey at the 2016 Summer Olympics – Women's tournament").[[1]](#cite_note-NBC-1)

The last edition of both the [men's](/wiki/Field_hockey_at_the_2024_Summer_Olympics_%E2%80%93_Men%27s_tournament "Field hockey at the 2024 Summer Olympics – Men's tournament") and [women's events](/wiki/Field_hockey_at_the_2024_Summer_Olympics_%E2%80%93_Women%27s_tournament "Field hockey at the 2024 Summer Olympics – Women's tournament") were held as a part of the [2024 Paris Olympics](/wiki/2024_Paris_Olympics "2024 Paris Olympics") with Netherlands winning both the events.[[8]](#cite_note-8)[[9]](#cite_note-9) India holds the record for the most appearances in the Olympic men's competitions (22) and [Spain](/wiki/Spain_men%27s_national_field_hockey_team "Spain men's national field hockey team") has appeared in the most Olympic men's competitions (19) without winning the men's gold medal with the best place finishes being the silver medal won in [1980](/wiki/Field_hockey_at_the_1980_Summer_Olympics "Field hockey at the 1980 Summer Olympics"), [1996](/wiki/Field_hockey_at_the_1996_Summer_Olympics "Field hockey at the 1996 Summer Olympics"), and [2008](/wiki/Field_hockey_at_the_2008_Summer_Olympics "Field hockey at the 2008 Summer Olympics").[[5]](#cite_note-India-5)[[10]](#cite_note-10) Australia and Netherlands hold the record for the most appearances in the Olympic women's competitions (11).[[3]](#cite_note-Olympedia-3) India leads the medal tally for the men's events with 13 medals including eight gold medals. Netherlands heads the women's events with 10 medals including five gold medals and also leads the overall medal tally with 20 medals (eight gold, six silver and six bronze).[[11]](#cite_note-11)[[3]](#cite_note-Olympedia-3)

## Notes

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Field_hockey_at_the_Summer_Olympics&action=edit&section=19 "Edit section: Notes")]

1. **[^](#cite_ref-12)** There was no bronze medal match for the 1908 Games in London.
2. ^ [***a***](#cite_ref-year_13-0) [***b***](#cite_ref-year_13-1) Three teams participated in the 1932 tournament, so they played a round-robin format.
3. **[^](#cite_ref-EUA_14-0)** The [United Team of Germany](/wiki/United_Team_of_Germany "United Team of Germany") was the combined team from [West Germany](/wiki/West_Germany "West Germany") and [East Germany](/wiki/German_Democratic_Republic "German Democratic Republic") that competed together at the Olympic Games from 1956 to 1964. Then each country sent independent teams from 1968 to 1988.
4. ^ [***a***](#cite_ref-India_15-0) [***b***](#cite_ref-India_15-1) [***c***](#cite_ref-India_15-2) Includes three gold medals as [British India](/wiki/British_India "British India")
5. **[^](#cite_ref-16)** Includes results representing the [United Team of Germany](/wiki/United_Team_of_Germany "United Team of Germany") from 1956 to 1964 and [West Germany](/wiki/West_Germany "West Germany") from 1968 to 1988
6. ^ [***a***](#cite_ref-moscow_17-0) [***b***](#cite_ref-moscow_17-1) Round-robin format play in 1980 for women's tournament
7. **[^](#cite_ref-18)** Round Robin format use in 1984 women's tournament
8. **[^](#cite_ref-19)** The final standings show both the United States and Australia were tied in points and had same margin in goal difference (both having scored 9 goals and conceded 7 goals), therefore a penalty stroke competition was played to decide the bronze medal winner, with the United States winning.
9. **[^](#cite_ref-20)** Includes representing [West Germany](/wiki/West_Germany "West Germany") from 1984 to 1988
